summary:
Started as a request to create a functional testing routine for CRM Builder YAML processing. Through iterative reframing during the conversation, the scope evolved into a substantially larger architectural redesign — a structured database as source of truth for all CRM implementation artifacts (methodology and project management both), with Word documents, deployment YAML, and test cases as renders.

Key reframes during the conversation, in order:

- Testing system → behavior-driven testing → cross-cutting persona/process testing → end-to-end PRD-to-deployed-CRM verification with feedback loop to Claude Code
- Word documents as source → structured database as source
- Project artifacts as markdown documents → project artifacts as database records (dogfooding)
- YAML files in git → SQLite + REST API + MCP server (real database)
- MCP-direct → layered (REST API as durable interface, MCP as Claude.ai bridge)
- Topics as one of many junction tables → universal references pattern with controlled relationship vocabulary

Working through the eight planning dimensions:

- Resolved #1 (project identity) including all sub-questions A–F
- Resolved #2.1 (session orientation protocol)
- Substantially resolved #2.3, #2.4, #3 by virtue of the database architecture
- Collapsed #4 (CBM coexistence) — CBM is the test case, not parallel commitment
- Did not address #5–#8 (deferred to future conversations)
title: Initial Planning — Project Identity, Architecture, and Initial Artifacts
topics_covered: v2 project identity, methodology rearchitecture rationale, storage architecture (SQLite + REST API + MCP server), universal references pattern, topics table, session orientation protocol, charter structure, decisions log structure, CBM as test case

summary:
Worked through the four sub-questions of planning dimension #5. A side discussion about whether the next iteration of CRMBuilder should move to a separate Claude Project (giving v2 design conversations a clean memory slate) or a separate GitHub repository was deferred to the end of planning, after dimensions #6 through #8 are resolved. The remaining sub-questions resolved cleanly:

- Anatomy of a working session: orient using the tiered orientation protocol established in the bootstrap, discuss in plain text one issue at a time, close with a Claude Code prompt that lands a commit. One Claude.ai conversation equals one session, which produces exactly one session record. Multi-conversation topics are reconstructed by reading the chronological sequence of session records that touch the topic. Every conversation that engages v2 produces a session record, even brief or exploratory ones.
- Cadence: ad-hoc. No fixed schedule. Sessions happen when the work is ready. Long gaps between sessions are expected; the "in-flight at session end" capture in each session record is the load-bearing mechanism for clean resumption.
- Milestones: the phase list already enumerated in the status document — schema design (Step 0), storage layer build (Step 1), MN domain migration as the proving ground (Step 2), then MR / CR / FU migrations in priority order, with test infrastructure layered in alongside.
- Definition of done and gating: Doug reviews each phase's output. Proceed if it works, iterate within the current step if it doesn't, stop and reassess if the approach can't be made to work. No formal checklist per phase; judgment based on whether the output works.

A communication rule was also added to Claude's stored memory during this session: Claude must never reference identifiers, codes, or shorthand tags without explaining what they mean in plain English. This is a working-style rule rather than a v2 architectural decision and is captured in stored memory rather than the v2 decisions log.
title: Planning Dimension #5 — Pacing, Cadence, and Milestones

summary:
Single-pass build of all eight acceptance criteria from the v0.1 PRD. Implementation plan was committed first (storage-system-implementation-plan.md), then status was updated to in-progress, then the build proceeded in the order: scaffold → schema (SQLAlchemy + Alembic baseline) → access layer + JSON export hook → access-layer test suite (53 tests) → REST API → API integration test suite (28 tests) → MCP server with FastMCP → MCP smoke test (5 tests) → bootstrap migration with parsers (10 tests) → run migration on real bootstrap files → retire markdown sources → close out via database (this session record).

Acceptance criteria 1–8 from the PRD's Section 8 all pass: schema deployed, access layer operational, REST API operational, MCP server operational, JSON exports generated atomically with DB writes, bootstrap content migrated, test suite passing (96 tests total), session orientation reads return content equivalent to the pre-migration markdown.

v2 work now operates on the database. Future updates to charter, status, decisions, and sessions go through the access layer (or the REST API or MCP) rather than markdown edits, with JSON snapshots committed alongside any DB-changing commit.

summary: The planning conversation worked through the seven architectural questions listed above and produced three artifacts. The v0.1 PRD (ui-PRD-v0.1.md) captures the requirements: a standalone PySide6 desktop application that consumes the storage system REST API, providing read-only views across all seven governance entities and the references graph, with full CRUD for decisions. The implementation plan (ui-implementation-plan.md) decomposes the build into eight independently testable slices (v2-ui-A through v2-ui-H), each delivered as its own Claude Code prompt. The v2-ui-A prompt is the first of these, scaffolding the package, registering the console script, adding a GET /health endpoint, and writing this session record and DEC-018 through DEC-024 into the v2 database to capture the planning conversation.
title: UI v0.1 planning
topics_covered: Seven architectural questions for the v2 desktop UI: (1) standalone PySide6 application versus a new tab embedded in the v1 PySide6 application; (2) transport choice — REST API over HTTP versus direct in-process imports of the access layer; (3) v0.1 scope boundary — read-only across all governance entities versus a broader CRUD surface, with full create/read/update/delete reserved for decisions only; (4) screen layout — sidebar navigation with master/detail panes versus top-tab and dashboard alternatives; (5) refresh strategy — file-watching the db-export/ snapshot directory as the change signal, with a manual Refresh button as fallback, in preference to polling or push; (6) server lifecycle — detect-then-launch QProcess management of the API subprocess so the UI cooperates with externally-launched API instances; (7) styling — native Qt look with a minimal QSS accent stub for v0.1, with a real designed pass deferred to v0.2.

artifacts_produced: Eight prompts under PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/ (CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-A through H). Source under crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/. ~25 test files under tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/ (264 v2 tests passing). Storage-system additions: GET /health endpoint (slice A), Alembic migration 0002_soft_delete_decisions, soft-delete semantics for decisions (slice H), 'Deleted' added to DECISION_STATUSES vocab.
in_flight_at_end: v0.2 backlog: write surfaces for non-decision entities (charter, status, sessions, risks, planning items, topics, references); reference rendering on Sessions/Risks/Charter/Status/Topics/Planning Items detail panes; charter/status replace flows with version history browsing; calendar widget on Decision Date input; QTreeView for Topics; full styling design pass per DEC-024; 'Show deleted' toggle on the decisions panel toolbar.
summary: UI v0.1 shipped: standalone PySide6 desktop application that consumes the storage system REST API. Eight read-only entity panels with master/detail navigation and cross-panel reference links. Full create/edit/delete for decisions, with client-side format validation (DEC-NNN identifier, MM-DD-YY date). File-watch live refresh with content-hash gating to suppress no-op rewrites from the snapshot exporter. Detect-then-launch API subprocess management. Soft-delete semantics added to decisions: DELETE sets status='Deleted', references to deleted decisions still resolve, list_all filters them out by default. _resolve_decision_id treats empty string as 'clear FK' so PATCH /decisions/{id} with supersedes='' works. About dialog wired to Help menu. Topics master-list 'Parent Topic' cell click navigation. Styling stub refined for navy accent + deep red error labels.
title: UI v0.1 build

Slice H also folded in two storage-system fixes (soft-delete decisions + PATCH FK-clear) required for the documented contract to hold end-to-end.

title: UI v0.2 planning
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Plan v0.2 of the v2 desktop UI for the CRM Builder project. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces three deliverables: (1) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.2.md — intent, scope, acceptance criteria, error handling matrix, open questions; (2) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-v0.2-implementation-plan.md — slice breakdown with deliverables and acceptance gates per slice; (3) Execution prompts under PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.2-{A..*}-*.md — one per implementation slice. The conversation that produces these deliverables is the v0.2 planning session and will be captured as SES-006 at the conversation's close."

The planning conversation worked through eight architectural questions in order:

1. Release shape — what kind of release v0.2 is. Three theses considered: complete the write surface (CRUD parity for non-decision entities), polish the read surface (full styling pass + reference rendering everywhere), or hybrid (writes for two entities + visual pass). Resolved as Thesis A (complete the write surface) with two carve-outs (calendar widget on date inputs, "Show deleted" toggle on Decisions). The full styling design pass per DEC-024 is deferred again, to v0.3.

2. Entity scope — which entities get which shape of write affordance. Resolved: Risks, Planning Items, Topics get full CRUD using a shared CRUD framework; Charter and Status get a versioned-replace flow with version-history browsing including Make Current; Sessions are excluded as a governance-level boundary per DEC-013/DEC-014; References are deferred to v0.3 as their own focused slice.

3. Dialog architecture — extract shared base classes versus per-entity duplicates. Resolved: extract EntityCrudDialog and EntityCrudDeleteDialog parameterized by a per-entity field schema; v0.1's decisions dialogs become the first user of the base. Charter/Status share a parallel VersionedReplaceDialog base.

4. Charter/Status payload editor + version-history UX. Resolved: raw JSON editor with Validate button, sufficient for v2's loose payload schema and infrequent-edit use case. Make Current affordance on non-current versions is included for v0.2 to support reverting.

5. Topics — tree view in master panel + parent picker in dialog. Resolved: QTreeView replacing the v0.1 indented QTableView. Parent picker is a reusable HierarchicalEntityPicker widget with cycle filtering via a selectable-predicate callback. The widget lands once and serves future hierarchical fields in methodology entities.

6. Reference rendering generalization. Resolved: shared ReferencesSection widget on every detail pane, both inbound and outbound, grouped by relationship type with counts, empty state with "(none)" placeholder, no filtering in v0.2. References-as-edges panel keeps its v0.1 list-only treatment with no detail pane.

7. Small batched items — calendar scope, Show-deleted toggle behavior, vocabulary handling. Resolved: DateField widget wrapping QDateEdit replaces every plain-text date input that v0.2 touches; Show-deleted is a checkbox toggle on the Decisions panel toolbar with strikethrough rendering for deleted rows and a Restore button on the detail pane for soft-deleted records; vocabularies for new entities are imported from access-layer vocab.py (RISK_PROBABILITIES/IMPACTS/STATUSES, PLANNING_ITEM_TYPES/STATUSES — all already present from v0.1).

8. Slice breakdown. Resolved: six slices (A foundation refactor, B Risks CRUD, C Planning Items CRUD, D Topics CRUD + QTreeView + HierarchicalEntityPicker, E Charter and Status replace + Sessions ReferencesSection, F Show-deleted + polish + closeout). A is the prerequisite for all subsequent slices; B/C/D could be parallelized in principle but are sequential in practice for single-developer execution.

artifacts_produced: Six prompts (CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.2-A through F) under PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/. Source under crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/ui/widgets/, base/crud_dialog.py, base/versioned_replace_dialog.py, plus per-entity dialogs and panel modifications. Test files under tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/ for each new dialog and panel write surface. Storage-system additions where required: API decisions list endpoint plumbed include_deleted query param through to the access layer's list_all (the access layer already supported it from v0.1 slice H); topics access fix special-cases parent_topic='' as 'clear FK' parallel to v0.1 slice H's supersedes='' fix; StorageClient gained restore_decision() convenience method. About dialog version reads from crmbuilder_v2.__version__ which is bumped to 0.2.0.
conversation_reference: Six Claude Code execution sessions per the CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.2-A through F prompt series. No transcripts preserved per DEC-025.
in_flight_at_end: v0.3 backlog: References write surface as its own focused slice (relationship vocabulary picker, source/target picker UX, edge-deletion semantics); Sessions write surface only if DEC-013/DEC-014 are revised to permit non-Claude session records; full styling design pass per DEC-024; ListDetailPanel master-pane factory refactor (slice F polish item 8 deferred); reference filtering by relationship type if reference volume warrants it; diff-with-current view for the JSON payload editor if friction surfaces; methodology entity panels post-schema-design; global search across entities; keyboard shortcuts beyond Qt defaults; export visible panel to CSV/JSON; bulk operations.
summary: UI v0.2 shipped: full create/edit/delete for Risks, Planning Items, and Topics; versioned replace with Make Current for Charter and Status; reference rendering on every detail pane via the shared ReferencesSection widget; calendar widget on date inputs (DateField); QTreeView master panel for Topics with reusable HierarchicalEntityPicker; Show-deleted toggle and Restore affordance on the Decisions panel. v0.1's tests continue to pass after the slice A refactor; total v2 test count grew to 458. Sessions and References write surfaces remain deferred to v0.3 per DEC-027.
title: UI v0.2 build
topics_covered: Six-prompt execution of the UI v0.2 build per ui-v0.2-implementation-plan.md: foundation refactor (A), risks CRUD (B), planning items CRUD (C), topics CRUD with QTreeView and HierarchicalEntityPicker (D), charter and status replace flows + sessions references section (E), show-deleted toggle + polish + closeout (F). Foundation refactor extracted EntityCrudDialog and EntityCrudDeleteDialog base classes plus a new widgets/ subpackage with DateField, ReferencesSection, and HierarchicalEntityPicker; v0.1's decisions dialogs migrated to use the base. Each subsequent slice instantiated the base for its entity, added the entity's panel-side write integration, and dropped ReferencesSection on the detail pane. Charter/Status got the parallel VersionedReplaceDialog with raw JSON editor and Make Current affordance. Slice F closed with the Show-deleted toggle + Restore affordance, About dialog version bump, README update, base-class polish (promoted _select_by_identifier, JSON inline error rendering), and this record.

title: UI v0.3 planning
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Plan v0.3 of the v2 desktop UI for the CRM Builder project. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces three deliverables: (1) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.3.md — intent, scope, acceptance criteria, error handling matrix, open questions; (2) PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-v0.3-implementation-plan.md — slice breakdown with deliverables and acceptance gates per slice; (3) Execution prompts under PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.3-{A..*}-*.md — one per implementation slice. The conversation that produces these deliverables is the v0.3 planning session and will be captured as SES-008 at the conversation's close."

The planning conversation worked through nine architectural questions in order:

1. Release shape and scope envelope. The kickoff prompt framed v0.3 as testability-first: after this release, the user intends to begin actually using v2 to drive governance work. Priority tiers proposed: Priority 1 (testability-blocking — References write, Sessions create), Priority 2 (architecturally-overdue — ListDetailPanel factory refactor), Priority 3 (independent quality — full styling pass), Priority 4 (nice-to-haves — reference filtering, JSON diff, global search, exports, bulk ops, methodology entities, keyboard shortcuts). Resolved: Priority 1 + Priority 2 in scope; Priority 3 + 4 out, with possible micro-adjustments inside the references slice for any visual convention the picker UX itself demands.

2. References write surface — entry points. Three options considered: panel-only, detail-pane + panel, detail-pane only. Resolved: detail-pane + panel (option B). The user added a global directive: right-click context menus across all panels in addition to UI elements. Confirmed retroactive sweep across all existing panels is in scope, not just the new v0.3 surfaces.

3. References picker UX — order and vocab strictness. Source-first picking order with strict RELATIONSHIP_TYPES vocab compliance, identifier picker as editable QComboBox + QCompleter on IDENTIFIER — title items. Source-first matches the dominant "I'm linking X to Y" workflow; strict compliance turns the dropdowns into vocab documentation; the autocomplete combo handles up to thousands of entries gracefully. Vocab is read at dialog-open time so new kinds appear without UI changes.

4. References edit and delete semantics. Resolved: no edit affordance (graph edges have no clean edit semantic that isn't equivalent to delete + create); hard-delete with confirmation modal showing the edge text. Soft-delete on references rejected — references aren't first-class governance content, tombstones bloat the panel monotonically, audit through git history is sufficient for derivative content.

5. Sessions create-only — governance interpretation. The kickoff prompt noted that DEC-013 / DEC-014 / DEC-027 collectively imply "Claude-as-writer" through DEC-027's interpretation, but DEC-013 and DEC-014 themselves never restrict the writer. Resolved: a new clarifying decision (DEC-034) makes the writer-side interpretation explicit and reverses it — user-authored sessions via the UI are permitted, append-only stays strict (no edit, no delete, no drafts), sessions remain narrow-scoped to Claude.ai conversations. Broadening to non-Claude conversations is a separate decision to make later if the need surfaces.

6. Sessions create dialog — content and shape. Nine fields: identifier (auto-assigned), session_date (DateField, defaults today), status (combo from SESSION_STATUSES, defaults Complete), title (line edit), summary (multi-line), topics_covered (multi-line with placeholder hinting at DEC-025 seed-prompt convention), artifacts_produced (multi-line), in_flight_at_end (multi-line, optional), conversation_reference (multi-line with placeholder hinting at DEC-025 descriptive-text convention). Single-scroll layout consistent with EntityCrudDialog pattern. No content-pattern enforcement on freeform fields — placeholders carry the convention. Right-click on session rows: Go to references / Copy identifier; no Edit / Delete / Restore.

7. ListDetailPanel master-pane factory refactor — scope and test discipline. Adopt the v0.2 slice F deferred spec verbatim: _create_master_widget factory method with QTableView default; QTreeView panels override. Add _build_context_menu factory method as cohesive companion (right-click is the global UX principle from question 2). Test discipline: targeted parity tests per panel (master-widget type assertion + context-menu factory smoke test). Slice A landing alongside planning records.

8. Styling pass per DEC-024 — go in v0.3 or defer again. Three prior deferrals would establish a permanence pattern, but testability is the v0.3 frame and styling is a different category of work. Resolved: defer again with concrete commitment closing the deferral loop — new decision (DEC-037) defers; planning item PI-NNN tracks for a future dedicated styling release; v0.3 scope-driven micro-adjustments allowed (e.g., colored pill on relationship-kind label, deleted-row strikethrough carryforward). Without PI-NNN the third deferral would be the same as the first two.

9. Slice breakdown. Resolved: five slices. A — foundation (planning records + ListDetailPanel factory refactor + Topics migration + per-panel parity tests). B — right-click context menus uniform across all existing panels. C — references write surface (EntityIdentifierPicker widget + create dialog + delete dialog + panel button + detail-pane Add reference + right-click delete). D — sessions create dialog. E — closeout (micro-adjustments + About 0.3.0 + README + SES-009 + status update to v1.0). The v0.3 build executed five Claude Code prompts (slices A through E) per the v0.3 implementation plan. Slice A landed planning records (SES-008, DEC-032 through DEC-037, PI-001), the ListDetailPanel master-widget and context-menu factory refactor, and the Topics panel migration to the new factory. Slice B swept right-click context menus across every existing entity panel. Slice C delivered the full References write surface — EntityIdentifierPicker widget, ReferenceCreateDialog with source-first cascading filters and strict relationship-rules vocab compliance, ReferenceDeleteDialog, panel toolbar New Reference button, and detail-pane Add reference affordance with right-click delete. Slice E shipped v0.3 — micro-visual adjustments observed during build, version bump to 0.3.0, About dialog refresh, README update, SES-009 (this record, written through the new Sessions create dialog as the user-facing acceptance test), and status update to v1.0 phase "v0.3 complete".

title: UI v0.3 build
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Execute the v2-ui-v0.3 build series in sequence: slice A foundation and factory refactor, slice B right-click context menus, slice C references write surface, slice D sessions create dialog, slice E closeout. Each slice has its own prompt under PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.3-{A..E}-*.md and is acceptance-gated independently. Do not skip ahead; do not collapse slices."

The build worked through five execution slices in order:

A — Foundation and factory refactor. Wrote planning records (SES-008, DEC-032 through DEC-037, PI-NNN, six decided_in references, status v0.9) per DEC-014 / DEC-025. Refactored ListDetailPanel with _create_master_widget and _build_context_menu factory methods. Migrated TopicsPanel to override _create_master_widget; removed the v0.2 self._table = self._tree workaround. Added per-panel parity tests for master-widget type and context-menu factory return type.

B — Right-click context menus across existing panels. Each of the eight existing entity panels (Decisions, Sessions, Risks, Planning Items, Topics, References, Charter, Status) overrode _build_context_menu with action sets paralleling the existing toolbar and detail-pane buttons. Action handlers reused existing slots — no new business logic introduced. Sweep test in test_context_menus.py asserts action-set parity per panel.

C — References write surface. New EntityIdentifierPicker widget under widgets/ for autocomplete identifier selection. ReferenceCreateDialog with source-first cascading filters and strict relationship-rules vocab compliance. ReferenceDeleteDialog with edge-text confirmation and cannot-be-undone notice. Cascading-filter framework strategy resolved: Option 1 — extended FieldSchema with optional depends_on and compute_options fields plus a new identifier_picker widget kind. The cascade-wiring methods (_wire_cascade and _refresh_dependent_fields with per-iteration state re-snapshot) and the QSignalBlocker guard around option mutation were debugged through a Qt signal-storm hang and fixed before integration. Naming alignment resolved: Option B variant — kept relationship as the canonical name throughout the API and UI (form key, client kwarg, payload field), with relationship_kind preserved only as the DB column name; the existing _row_dict translation at the access layer boundary is the single translation point. Storage-layer additions: RELATIONSHIP_RULES tuple-keyed dict (source_type, target_type) → frozenset[kinds] in access/vocab.py with seven semantic-rule helpers (kinds_for_source, target_types_for, source_types_with_relationships); delete_by_id on the access-layer references repository; DELETE /references/{id} REST endpoint; create_reference and delete_reference on the UI's StorageClient. Panel and detail-pane integration: New Reference toolbar button on the References panel, Add reference affordance on every detail pane's ReferencesSection (opt-in via the client parameter so read-only embedders continue to work), right-click Delete reference on rows in both surfaces.

D — Sessions create-only dialog. SessionCreateDialog as instance of EntityCrudDialog with the nine-field schema per PRD §2.4: identifier (auto-assigned, read-only), session_date (default today), status (default Complete), title, summary, topics_covered (with DEC-025 placeholder), artifacts_produced, in_flight_at_end (optional), conversation_reference (with DEC-025 placeholder). Identifier auto-assignment via compute_next_session_identifier free function (extracted for unit-testability without the qtbot fixture). EntityCrudDialog framework extensions made: FieldSchema.read_only flag (always read-only, distinct from existing read_only_on_edit), placeholder support extended to text widgets (was line-only), default support extended to line widgets in create mode (was combo-only) — all additive, no ripple to existing dialogs. Sessions panel toolbar New Session button; whitespace right-click New session. Detail pane verified to have no Edit, Delete, or Restore button — append-only stays strict per DEC-013 / DEC-034 (asserted via blanket findChildren(QPushButton) scans excluding the ReferencesSection subtree, whose per-row Delete affordance targets references not the session record).

E — Closeout. Decisions panel context menu gained a menu.addSeparator() between the write actions (Edit / Delete or Restore) and the read action (Show references), mirroring the visual hierarchy already in place on the References panel from slice C; the existing Decisions context-menu tests were updated to filter out separators (matching the References pattern) and a new test asserts the separator placement. Friction polish: the F402 in base/crud_dialog.py (the clear_all_errors loop variable shadowed the imported field from dataclasses — renamed to field_key) and the two F821 in tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_client.py (added missing from typing import Any) were fixed. CLAUDE.md gained a note under the v2 methodology rearchitecture section explaining that REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS plus _kinds_for_pair (precomputing RELATIONSHIP_RULES from seven semantic rules) is the canonical relationship-rules location; adding a new kind requires updating both (plus an Alembic CHECK migration). Other Step 1 candidates were considered and deferred: no friction surfaced motivating the colored pill on the relationship-kind label, the Add-reference button placement was already settled in slice C (button at the bottom of the section), and the View-payload modal was already implemented in slice B. Version: crmbuilder_v2.__version__ bumped from 0.2.0 to 0.3.0; the About dialog renders 0.3.0 via the existing importlib.metadata → package-__version__ fallback path; the version test was updated. README: a new "v0.3 (current — closes the testability gap)" section under User interface describes References create/delete + Sessions create + global right-click + factory refactor; the v0.2 section lost its "current" marker but was otherwise preserved. SES-009 written through the new Sessions create dialog as the user-facing acceptance test for v0.3 (no script). Status update to version_label 1.0, phase "v0.3 complete", with the v0.4 candidate list folded in.

title: v0.3 planning chat supplement (slice review + v0.4 kickoff prep)
topics_covered:
This is a supplemental record to SES-008. SES-008 captured the v0.3 planning portion of the conversation up through the nine architectural questions and the six decisions DEC-032 through DEC-037. This supplement captures the post-SES-008 work in the same Claude.ai conversation.

Per DEC-013, one Claude.ai conversation = one session record. SES-008 was written prematurely (in slice A's planning records) and could not be amended (append-only). This supplement closes the audit gap. Future planning conversations adopt the session-record-at-close pattern (per the amended v0.4 planning kickoff prompt) so the gap does not recur.

The post-SES-008 work covered:

1. Verify-first API health check across all five slice prompts. The original prompts instructed unconditional 'uv run crmbuilder-v2-api &'; updated to verify-first via curl, only background-start on failure, re-check after ~3 seconds, stop and report if still failing. Single commit (53dea00).

2. Slice C prompt updates after slice A's report flagged two issues. (a) Slice A extended the _create_master_widget contract to allow factories to pre-install a model on the returned widget — the original prompt under-specified this, and Topics exercises the extended contract. Added Step 0 (preliminary docstring commit) to slice C so the contract is documented before slice C's heavy work begins. (b) Slice A surfaced a pre-existing v0.2 naming inconsistency between API field name (relationship) and DB column name (relationship_kind) that slice C would trip over. Added a 'Naming alignment' subsection to slice C Step 1 with two options for the slice to resolve. Single commit (154c876).

3. Slice E prompt updates after slice C and D reports. Filled the SES-009 placeholders with actual decisions: cascading-filter framework Option 1 (extended FieldSchema with depends_on / compute_options + identifier_picker widget kind); naming alignment Option B variant (relationship canonical in API/UI, relationship_kind canonical in DB, single translation point at access-layer _row_dict); RELATIONSHIP_RULES seven semantic rules; three EntityCrudDialog framework extensions slice D added (FieldSchema.read_only, placeholder support on text widgets, default support on line widgets in create mode); PI-NNN resolved to PI-001; test count updated to ~150 new tests. Added Step 4a for the CLAUDE.md note about relationship-rules location, Step 4b for opportunistic fix of three pre-existing lint warnings (F402 in crud_dialog.py, two F821 in test_client.py). Single commit (31580c5).

4. v0.4 planning kickoff prompt drafted at PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-v0.4-planning-prompt.md. Mirrors the v0.3 kickoff structure with three substantive differences: goal posture deliberately open (the first architectural question of the planning conversation, multiple framings plausible — PI-001 discharge as primary, polish from real-use friction, methodology entity schema design, or hybrid); PI-001 framed as forcing function (three deferrals via DEC-024 / DEC-026 / DEC-037 created the planning item explicitly so the third deferral did not silently drift to a fourth — v0.4 must engage explicitly, no silent rollover); production-use friction noted as the highest-weight input bucket (populated dynamically by sessions written between v0.3 close and v0.4 planning, advisory note that the conversation may benefit from running after some weeks of v0.3 production use). Single commit (58f3b2f).

5. v0.3 closeout review and governance follow-up. Slice E reported all 16 PRD acceptance criteria checked, 610 tests passing, SES-009 written through the new Sessions dialog as the user-facing acceptance test for v0.3. Three deviations addressed: (a) version source location (no crmbuilder-v2/pyproject.toml exists; v2 is bundled into parent crmbuilder distribution's root pyproject.toml with crmbuilder_v2.__version__ as the canonical source — added one-paragraph note to crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md so future v0.4+ closeout slices know); (b) selection-model navigation regression caught during live AC walk and fixed with selection-preserve-across-refresh logic plus a forward-guard test (validates the manual verification step in closeout slice prompts — automated coverage is a floor not a ceiling); (c) SES-008 partial-capture governance issue — resolved with this supplemental record (path-a fix) plus an amendment to the v0.4 planning kickoff specifying session-record-at-close pattern (path-b prevention). CLAUDE.md and v0.4 kickoff updates landed in commit f284368.

6. Write method correction. This supplemental record was initially proposed as a dialog paste (mirroring SES-009's dogfood pattern). On reflection: the dialog write was v0.3's testability acceptance test, a one-time demonstration that the dialog can author records — not an ongoing constraint that all future records must route through the dialog. DEC-034 only constrains record content and append-only semantic, not write method. For governance patches and other non-acceptance-test records, MCP / REST / script writes are equally legitimate. This record was written via direct API POST.

title: v0.4 planning — methodology entity schema-design workstream kickoff
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Plan v0.4 of the v2 desktop UI for the CRM Builder project. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces three deliverables: ui-PRD-v0.4.md (intent, scope, acceptance criteria, error handling matrix, open questions, same shape as v0.1/v0.2/v0.3), ui-v0.4-implementation-plan.md (slice breakdown with deliverables and acceptance gates per slice), and execution prompts under PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-v2-ui-v0.4-{A..*}-*.md (one per implementation slice, structure matching the v0.1/v0.2/v0.3 prompts). Cadence matches v0.1/v0.2/v0.3: structured architectural discussion driven one decision at a time, building toward the PRD first, then the implementation plan, then the execution prompts."

Twelve architectural questions discussed and resolved:

Q1 (frame): v0.4's primary frame. Doug surfaced that the natural next step was preparing for a real-world test by redoing the CBM design, and CBM redo on v2 as system of record for both governance and methodology content requires v2 to have entity types for methodology content. Path chosen: (b) — CBM redo runs on v2 as system of record for both governance AND methodology content; v0.4's primary frame becomes methodology entity schema design (Bucket C from the original kickoff). Captured in DEC-038.

Q1.5 (path under b): the original kickoff explicitly forbids designing schemas inline in a planning conversation. Three paths under (b) considered: (b-α) two-stage — planning conversation pivots to workstream kickoff; (b-β) placeholder v0.4 PRD with TBD schema slots; (b-γ) scope-only inline schema design (violates kickoff constraint). Chose (b-α). Captured in DEC-038.

Q2 (scope philosophy): three options — big design up front (8-10 entity types), minimum viable (only what evolved Phase 1 needs), hybrid (Phase 1 plus buffer). Chose minimum viable. Captured in DEC-038.

Q3 (methodology choice for CBM redo): two options — original 13-phase methodology (live, currently mid-execution on CBM) or evolved methodology (research-stage at PRDs/process/research/evolved-methodology/, simulator-tested against CBM on 04-30-26). Chose evolved methodology — adoption pilot doubles as v2 real-world test. Captured in DEC-038.

Q4 (multi-tenancy): kickoff forbids fundamental storage architecture changes, putting multi-tenant v2 out of scope. Finding (not a decision): one v2 instance per engagement. CBM gets its own v2 instance; CBM's Mission Statement uses the existing Charter entity (versioned-replace + Make Current pattern) — no new entity type for Mission Statement. Captured in DEC-039.

Q5 (entity inventory): Phase 1 produces Mission Statement (→ Charter), Domain Inventory, Prioritized Backbone, Initial CRM Candidate Set. Initial proposal: three entity types — domain, process, crm_candidate. Doug pushed back that excluding entity while including crm_candidate was strange. Phase 1 interview guide v0.2 line 62 confirms: 'Phase 1 may surface entity names as nouns the client uses, but does not produce Entity PRDs.' Revised: four entity types — domain, entity (thin), process (thin), crm_candidate. Persona deferred (Phase 1 guide explicitly excludes persona elicitation). Captured in DEC-039.

Q6 (per-conversation product): what does each schema-design conversation produce? Three options — (1) design only, (2) design plus build prompt, (3) design plus integration analysis. Chose (1): each schema-design conversation produces a schema spec, the DEC-NNN decisions made during it, and a session record; build prompts come from a separate v0.4-build-planning conversation that takes all four specs as input. Captured in DEC-040.

Q7 (conversation order): domain → entity → process → crm_candidate. Reasoning: domain is foundational; entity is independent of process; process is most relational; crm_candidate is independent coda. Alternative (crm_candidate first as warmup) rejected because warmup-first delays testing the spec methodology against relational complexity. Captured in DEC-040.

Q8 (schema-spec methodology doc outline): nine sections — Identity, Fields, Relationships, Lifecycle, API surface, UI considerations, Acceptance criteria, Open questions, Cross-references. Confirmed.

Q9 (UI considerations section pattern): template-with-deviation-by-justification — default panel layout given, schema may diverge with explicit rationale. Captured in DEC-040.

Q10 (existing v0.4 kickoff supersession): three options — (X) mark in place, (Y) delete, (Z) archive. Chose (X) — supersession header at the top of the existing kickoff; SES-010's artifacts_produced reference remains valid. Captured in DEC-041.

Q11 (PI-001 fourth deferral mechanics): kickoff requires fourth deferral to have new tracking mechanism plus rationale. Three options — (α) simple tracking, (β) CBM-redo-friction trigger, (γ) hard backstop. Chose (β) — if CBM redo Phase 1 surfaces visual friction on any of the four new methodology panels, PI-001 gets pulled to v0.5 ahead of any other v0.5 candidate. Captured in DEC-042.

Q12 (SES-010 identifier-asymmetry resolution): three options — defer entirely, GET helpers for new four only, GET helpers for all 12 prefixed entity types (with PI-002 tracking option C for future). Chose option (3): all 12. Captured in DEC-043.

Final synthesis: deliverables of this conversation confirmed as the workstream plan, schema-spec methodology guide, four per-entity kickoff prompts, supersession edit, six consolidated decisions, one PI patch, four new planning items.

title: domain schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #1
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the `domain` entity type schema for v2's storage layer. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces one deliverable: `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/domain.md` — the complete schema specification per the template in `methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md`. The directory does not yet exist; this conversation creates it."

Pre-flight reading completed: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (root); methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (master); methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (template); evolved-methodology-phase-outline.md (Phase 1 outputs); phase-1-interview-guide.md (Domain Inventory output spec, section 7.2); db-export decisions and sessions snapshots through SES-011 / DEC-043.

Nine architectural questions resolved in order:

Q1 — Identifier prefix. Adopted DOM (3 letters, matches existing v2 governance-entity norm DEC/SES/RSK/TOP/REF/CHR/STA). Affirmed soft-3-letter posture for the workstream rather than locking strict 3 — downstream specs (process, crm_candidate) retain flexibility per spec guide's 3-5 range where ambiguity warrants 4 letters. → DEC-044.

Q2 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Adopted two-content-field split (domain_purpose + domain_description) rather than collapsing to one description field, preserving Phase 1 output shape and separating priority-test artifact from work content. Added optional domain_notes as consultant scratchpad. Skipped mnemonic short_code field (deferred to PI-007); plain text for description (markdown deferred to CBM-redo signal); flat optional notes (structured journal deferred). → DEC-045.

Q3 follow-up — Parent-prefix field-naming convention. User-introduced cross-spec convention change: prefix common/generic fields with parent entity name to prevent confusion. Resolved (a) scope: option iii — applies to fields common across many entity types but not entity-specific by nature, with the realization that all domain v0.4 fields are common-or-could-be-common so all fields are prefixed including identifier and timestamps; (b) retroactivity: applies to existing governance entities too, but retrofit conflicts with (b-α) discipline and is tracked separately as PI-006; (c) identifier and timestamps: also get prefixed. Spec guide section 6 amendment queued for v0.4-build planning. → DEC-046.

Q3 — Status lifecycle. Adopted three values (candidate / confirmed / deferred) with default-candidate starter. Implemented one-way propose-verify gate (no regression from confirmed or deferred back to candidate). Rejection handled via soft-delete rather than a rejected status value; no archived status. Established cross-spec principle: status tracks engagement-scope lifecycle; soft-delete tracks existence-in-the-record. → DEC-047.

Q4 — Field-level validation specifics. No storage-level length caps on text fields in v0.4 (UI placeholders provide soft guidance; caps easy to add via migration later). Case-insensitive uniqueness on domain_name within the engagement. Flat plain-text domain_notes (no structured journal in v0.4). → Folded into DEC-045.

Q5 — Relationships. No outgoing relationships from domain in v0.4 (no FKs, no hierarchy, no source-side references-entity use). Inbound relationship kinds entity_scopes_to_domain and process_belongs_to_domain declared in source-side specs (entity.md and process.md), not in domain.md; domain.md lists them informationally in section 3.3.2. Mechanism choice (direct FK vs references-entity edge) deferred to source-side specs. Established {source}_{verb}_{target} naming pattern for methodology relationship-kind vocab; governance vocab unchanged. → DEC-048.

Q6 — API surface. Adopted standard cross-spec endpoint set with no deviations (8 endpoints including /next-identifier helper per DEC-043). Server-side status-transition validation returning HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition error body. Only ?include_deleted=true list filter in v0.4; additional filters deferred to CBM-redo signal. → Folded into DEC-049.

Q7 — UI considerations. Adopted spec guide default ListDetailPanel layout with no architectural deviations. New 'Methodology' sidebar group below Governance with domain first (entity, process, crm_candidate following in workstream order; all four shipping together in v0.4). Master columns Identifier/Name/Status/Updated. Detail pane with domain_notes under collapsible 'Internal notes' header collapsed by default. → Folded into DEC-049.

Q8 — Acceptance criteria. Drafted 14 numbered testable statements covering schema migration, access-layer methods, REST endpoints, identifier-auto-assignment concurrency, soft-delete/restore round-trip, UI structural elements, CRUD round-trip, file-watch refresh, and end-to-end CBM-redo Phase 1 authoring through the dialog. → Folded into DEC-049.

Q9 — Open questions and cross-references. Catalogued three v0.4-build-settle items (spec guide section 6 amendment; source-side mechanism choice for inbound relationships; concurrent identifier-assignment behavior), six CBM-redo-surface items (markdown for description/purpose; storage length caps; structured notes journal; one-way gate friction; archived status; server-side list filters), and five v0.5+ planning items (PI-003, PI-004, PI-005 existing; PI-006 retrofit and PI-007 short_code new this conversation). Consolidated nine question outcomes into six decisions (DEC-044 through DEC-049) rather than nine separate records, mirroring SES-011's consolidation pattern.

Outline approved and end-to-end drafting executed without per-section confirmation. Spec committed to main at 295b37f. Close-out workflow simplified mid-conversation: replaced SES-012-specific apply script with generic apply_close_out.py that processes a single JSON payload (all records including session) via direct API. PI-008 proposed for the architectural fix: build inbox folder watcher into the v0.3 desktop application so future close-outs require no script execution at all.

title: entity schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #2
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the `entity` entity type schema for v2's storage layer. The 'entity' here is the CRM-modeled noun — Contact, Account, Session, Engagement, Dues — the things the eventual CRM stores records about. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces one deliverable: `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/entity.md` — the complete schema specification per the template in `methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md`. Ship thin in v0.4 — name, brief description, status — designed with growth path in mind for v0.5+ when Phase 3 demands attach fields, relationships, and full PRD content."

Pre-flight reading completed: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (root, including the v2 reference vocabulary mechanics at line 48); methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (master, noting line 168 names ENT-NNN as the working assumption per DEC-043); methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (template); methodology-schema-specs/domain.md v1.0 (predecessor spec, 379 lines, source of inherited conventions); phase-1-interview-guide.md v0.2 (line 62 entity-definition scope; section 7.2 Domain Inventory output spec excluding candidate entities; section 4.3 between-sessions discipline); close-out-payloads/ses_012.json (DEC-044..049 + PI-006/007/008 from the domain conversation; payload exists but db-export snapshots through SES-011/DEC-043 confirm it has not yet been applied — numbers in this payload assume SES-012 lands first); decisions.json snapshot (DEC-038/039/043 read in detail for context on derived-fields, minimum-viable inventory rationale, identifier-asymmetry resolution).

Eight architectural questions resolved in order:

Q1 — Identifier prefix and format. Affirmed ENT under domain.md's soft-3-letter posture (DEC-044). Three letters, unambiguous, no collision with existing prefixes (DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM), matches v2 governance-entity norm, already named as the working assumption in DEC-043 and workstream plan line 168. → DEC-050.

Q2 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Adopted a single content field (entity_description) rather than mirroring domain's two-content-field split (purpose + description). Reasoning: domain_purpose answers a priority-test mission question that has no analog for entity — an entity is a noun the client uses, and the 'why this entity matters to the engagement' question is answered relationally by its entity_scopes_to_domain references, not by free-text purpose. Adding entity_purpose would duplicate entity_description or invent a methodology concept Phase 1 doesn't produce. Five substantive fields plus inherited timestamps: entity_identifier, entity_name, entity_description, entity_notes (optional), entity_status. No entity_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (Phase 3 territory; deferred under PI-010). No storage-level length caps (UI soft guidance only, matching domain). Case-insensitive entity_name uniqueness within the engagement. Parent-prefix field naming inherited from DEC-046 without re-establishment. → DEC-051.

Q3 — Status lifecycle. Adopted domain's three-status pattern unchanged (candidate / confirmed / deferred), one-way propose-verify gate, freely bidirectional between confirmed and deferred, rejection via soft-delete, no archived status. Documented the entity-specific status-independence-from-affiliation-status posture: an entity's entity_status is its own field, set by the consultant based on client verification of the entity itself, not derived from the statuses of the domains the entity scopes to. This independence matters because an entity may legitimately span domains at different lifecycle positions (Contact in CBM might scope to MN/MR confirmed and FU deferred simultaneously, and Contact's own status is confirmed because the client agreed Contact is a thing in their world). Server-side transition validation returning HTTP 422 with invalid_status_transition error body, identical to domain. Noted as a future v0.5+ open question: a separate 'defined' or 'specified' status may surface once fields land, or the references-table cardinality between entity and field may suffice as the implicit signal. → DEC-052.

Q4 — Domain affiliation mechanism. The architecturally consequential question. Considered three candidates: (A) references-entity edge with new entity_scopes_to_domain relationship kind, (B) multi-value FK column on entity table, (C) direct single-value FK. (C) rejected outright — contradicts the methodology reality that entities span domains (CBM Contact lives in MN/MR/FU simultaneously). (B) considered but rejected — breaks discipline with v2's references-first posture (DEC-006), reverse queries require scanning all entity rows, FK validation reinvents what the references mechanism handles, and future relationship kinds on entity (entity-touches-process, entity-has-field, entity-has-variant) would each need their own multi-value columns, proliferating schema. Adopted (A). Many-to-many cardinality with no upper bound on either side. Zero-affiliation explicitly permitted (Phase 1 sometimes surfaces an entity name before its domain scoping is settled; the references discipline must not force pre-decided affiliation). Soft-deleting either endpoint does not cascade-delete references; restore restores them in place. The existing ReferencesSection widget renders the affiliations in the detail pane for free. → DEC-053 (combines mechanism and vocabulary registration since they are inseparable architectural choices).

Q5 — Relationship-kind vocabulary additions. Single new vocab entry: entity_scopes_to_domain (source=entity, target=domain). Conforms to the {source}_{verb}_{target} naming pattern inherited from DEC-048 — naming convention adopted not re-decided. Three coordinated mechanical additions per CLAUDE.md line 48: add entry to REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS in vocab.py; extend _kinds_for_pair so (entity, domain) returns {entity_scopes_to_domain}; Alembic migration extending the refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint to include the new value. No other vocab additions in this spec; inbound kinds (process_touches_entity, field_belongs_to_entity, entity_variant_of_entity) are anticipated and documented informationally in section 3.3.2 but declared by their source-side specs (process.md and future v0.5+ specs), not by this one. → Folded into DEC-053.

Q6 — API surface. Adopted standard cross-spec endpoint set with no deviations (8 endpoints including /entities/next-identifier helper per DEC-043). Server-side status-transition validation identical to domain's pattern. Only ?include_deleted=true list filter in v0.4. Decomposed reference handling explicitly called out: domain affiliations are NOT inlined into entity create/update bodies; the client makes separate POST /references calls with source/target/kind. This keeps the entity API consistent with v2's references-first discipline (DEC-006) and matches how the v0.3 UI handles references for governance entities; the New dialog hides the two-call sequence behind a single user gesture but the API stays decomposed. No shortcut endpoints. → Folded into DEC-054.

Q7 — UI considerations. Adopted spec guide default ListDetailPanel layout with no architectural deviations. Methodology sidebar group position #2 (after Domains, before Processes and CRM Candidates which ship together in v0.4). Standard four-column master pane (identifier, name, status, updated). One real deferral: a Domains-column on the master pane was considered for v0.4 since entity-to-domain affiliation is the entity's defining classification, but deferred to v0.5+ paired with PI-007 short codes. Without short codes, the column would render DOM-001/002/003 — identifiers that mean nothing at a glance; with short codes (MN/MR/FU), the column becomes high-value. The N+1 / batched-join query design isn't free for a column whose value the detail pane already exposes one click away via ReferencesSection. Tracked as new PI-009. Detail pane shows fields in section-3.2 order with entity_notes under a collapsible 'Internal notes' header collapsed by default (mirrors domain). Create dialog's domain-affiliation flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as an open question for v0.4-build planning to decide since both satisfy the acceptance criterion that the user can attach affiliations without leaving the UI. → Folded into DEC-054.

Q8 — Acceptance criteria. Drafted 16 numbered testable statements: 13 mirror domain's structural set (schema migration, identifier format constraint, name uniqueness, status enum/transition validation, access-layer methods, REST endpoint round-trip, identifier-auto-assignment concurrency, soft-delete/restore, sidebar entry, master pane columns, detail pane fields, CRUD dialogs, file-watch refresh) with entity field-name substitutions, plus three specifically covering the new vocabulary registration and references mechanism: (14) entity_scopes_to_domain registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS and constrained correctly at _kinds_for_pair and refs.relationship_kind CHECK constraint; (15) references created and queryable bidirectionally with persistence across soft-delete/restore of either endpoint; (16) sample CBM-redo Phase 1 records authored through the UI including domain affiliations. → Folded into DEC-054.

Outline approved and end-to-end drafting executed without per-section confirmation. Spec committed to main at 40e2cf5. Consolidated eight question outcomes into five decisions (DEC-050 through DEC-054) — narrower than domain's six because the cross-spec conventions established by SES-012 (parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046; {source}_{verb}_{target} relationship-kind naming per DEC-048) carry forward unchanged and are adopted not re-decided in this spec.

Two new planning items authored: PI-009 master-pane Domains column on Entities panel (jointly enabled by PI-007 short codes); PI-010 entity-schema v0.5+ extensions covering variants (Mentor Contact / Client Contact pattern; mechanism TBD) and base-type/kind classification (person / organization / event / transaction). Tracked separately from PI-004 (which groups other deferred entity types) because these are entity-schema-growth, not new entity types.

Five decisions:

- DEC-056: process field inventory under v0.4 scope (three content fields, no description-distinct-from-purpose, engagement-global name uniqueness, no status field deviation)

title: process schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #3
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the process entity type schema for v2's storage layer. A 'process' in the methodology sense is a sequence of work the organization does to advance its mission — Client Intake, Mentor Onboarding, Annual Dues Cycle, etc. — the things that get full Process Documents in Phase 3 of the evolved methodology and that constitute the Prioritized Backbone in Phase 1. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces one deliverable: PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/process.md — the complete schema specification per the template in methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md."

Pre-flight reading completed: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (root, including the v2 reference vocabulary mechanics at line 48); methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (master); methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (template); methodology-schema-specs/domain.md v1.0 (first predecessor, 379 lines, source of inherited conventions); methodology-schema-specs/entity.md v1.0 (second predecessor, 452 lines, source of references-decomposition pattern and master-pane-column-deferral pattern); close-out-payloads/ses_012.json (DEC-044..049 + PI-006/007/008 from domain, applied); close-out-payloads/ses_013.json (DEC-050..054 + PI-009/010 from entity, payload exists but not yet applied per db-export snapshot — numbers in this SES-014 payload assume SES-013 lands first); phase-1-interview-guide.md v0.2 (section 3.5 Part C process surfacing; section 4.1 between-session drafting with workability check; section 7.3 Prioritized Backbone output spec); evolved-methodology-phase-outline.md (Principle 3 priority-at-process-level; Principle 7 priority-not-locked; Phase 1 / Phase 3 boundary).

Architectural questions resolved:

Q1 — Identifier prefix and format. Adopted PROC (four letters) invoking domain.md's soft-3-letter posture explicit deviation clause. PRC rejected for ambiguity (reads as typo, no natural disambiguation). PR collides with public relations and is two letters. P too short. No collision with DEC/SES/RSK/PI/TOP/REF/CHR/STA/DOM/ENT. → DEC-055.

Q2 — Priority vs. status: two fields or one. The kickoff prompt's working set (candidate / confirmed / deprecated / deferred) conflated two orthogonal concepts: methodology priority classification (per Principle 3) and propose-verify record lifecycle. After clarifying that the user reframed the lifecycle question as definitional-completeness rather than existence-verification ("in Phase 1 a process is identified and an overview provided; in later phases the process is refined / detailed / confirmed to be complete"), resolved that v0.4 schema has only the identified level and therefore no status field — a status field with one value is no field at all. The methodology's deferred semantic that status carries for domain and entity is captured for process by classification=deferred instead; overloading both fields would create field-collision ambiguity. Rejection continues to be handled by soft-delete per cross-spec precedent. → contributes to DEC-056 (no-status deviation) and DEC-057 (classification field).

Q3 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Three substantive content fields: process_purpose (required, one-sentence — explicitly produced in Phase 1 7.3 output for backbone processes), process_classification_rationale (optional — priority-test answer for mission-critical; one-line reason for supporting and deferred), process_notes (optional, internal scratchpad). No separate process_description (purpose carries the work-content meaning). No process_kind base-type classification in v0.4 (Phase 3 territory; deferred under PI-005). No storage-level length caps. Engagement-global case-insensitive uniqueness on process_name. Direct FK to domain via process_domain_identifier (required). → contributes to DEC-056.

Q4 — Process classification: term and values. User pushed back on the working name 'priority' because conventional priorities are scalar (Critical/High/Medium/Low) while the methodology's three buckets (mission-critical / supporting / deferred) are a fused classification mixing importance, scope, and role rather than a scalar. Adopted process_classification as the field name and unclassified | mission_critical | supporting | deferred as the enum values, with unclassified default reflecting the Session-1-capture-before-classification state. One-way out of unclassified; free movement among the three classified values per Principle 7. Future scalar implementation-priority field deferred and tracked as a new PI. → DEC-057. Future scalar tracked as PI-011.

Q5 — Domain affiliation mechanism. Direct FK column on process table (process_domain_identifier), not references-table edge. Cardinality settled at the methodology level (many-to-one). No vocab.py registration — the references vocab governs the references table only, and a direct FK doesn't produce references rows. Domain.md section 3.3.2's anticipated relationship name (process_belongs_to_domain) stays informational only. → contributes to DEC-058.

Q6 — Process-to-process connections: directional or undirected, name. Directional, per the methodology's handoff language in Phase 1 guide section 4.1 ("Process A hands off to Process B if A produces records or state that B requires") and section 7.3 output (handoffs to/from other backbone processes — both sides named distinctly). Source = producer; target = consumer. Relationship kind name: process_hands_off_to_process. Registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS and _kinds_for_pair; Alembic migration extends CHECK constraint. → contributes to DEC-058.

Q7 — Process-to-entity touches in v0.4: defer entirely or register vocab now. Deferred entirely. PI-005 (process schema growth beyond Phase 1 thin shape) already tracks this. Pre-committing to a coarse vocab name (process_touches_entity) risks ossifying the wrong name when Phase 3 may need finer-grained kinds (read / create / update / delete). The spec's anticipated-inbound-relationships table (section 3.3.2) lists process_touches_entity as v0.5+ vocab without registration. → contributes to DEC-058.

Q8 — Process name uniqueness: per-(domain, name) or engagement-global. Initially proposed per-(domain, name) to permit natural client-language names like 'Recruit' in both MR and CR domains. User pushed back asking what documentation would show for a per-domain unique name and applied the principle 'if there could ever be user confusion, then we should go engagement specific.' The handoff lists in Phase 1 output documents reference process names alone without domain context, creating ambiguity risk under per-domain uniqueness. Adopted engagement-global case-insensitive uniqueness mirroring domain/entity precedent; consultants use suffixed names (Mentor Recruit, Client Recruit) as CBM already does today. → contributes to DEC-056.

Q9 — API surface, UI defaults, acceptance criteria. Standard endpoint set with no deviations. Decomposed reference handling for handoffs (no inline-handoff convenience endpoints), matching entity.md decomposed posture for affiliations. UI: Methodology sidebar group position #3 (Domains, Entities, Processes, CRM Candidates); master pane columns Identifier / Name / Classification / Updated (Domain column deferred to v0.5+ paired with PI-007, mirroring entity.md's posture); detail pane renders bidirectional process_hands_off_to_process references in separate Hands-off-to and Receives-from sub-sections; create-dialog handoff flow (create-then-attach vs create-with-attach) left as v0.4-build decision matching the open question on entity affiliations. 15 testable acceptance criteria. → DEC-059.

Mid-conversation working-cadence calibration. Identified that the conversation's own dialog mode (per-section permission requests on every architectural sub-question, then over-correcting to flat-weight consolidated proposals without per-decision depth) was not serving the user. Produced two paste-ready prompts to be run in a separate session to update userPreferences, project-level instructions, and kickoff-prompt templates: (1) a primary prompt defining three named modes (detail / architecture / prototype) with cadence rules and a default mode declaration mechanism; (2) a follow-on prompt establishing the architecture-mode decision template — eight elements: plain-language question, concrete example, each option with concrete behavior, why it matters, cost of recommendation honestly named, recommendation with rationale, follow-on detail separated, decision request with named options. The two prompts together provide the long-term fix; the user runs them in a separate session before the next CRMBuilder conversation.

title: crm_candidate schema design — methodology entity schema workstream conversation #4
topics_covered:
Seed prompt: "Design the `crm_candidate` entity type schema for v2's storage layer. A `crm_candidate` is one of the two or three CRM products selected at evolved Phase 1 for multi-deploy through the iteration loop — open source vs. commercial, hosting choices, budget, integrations, team-IT capabilities. The set persists across all iterations and is the basis for the final CRM selection in Phase 5. Drive a structured architectural discussion that produces two deliverables: `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/methodology-schema-specs/crm_candidate.md` — the complete schema specification per the template in `methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md`; and a v0.4-build-planning kickoff prompt at `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/ui-PRD-v0.4-build-planning-kickoff.md` that seeds the fifth and final workstream conversation. Fourth and final per-entity schema-design conversation; workstream's coda."

Pre-flight reading completed: crmbuilder/CLAUDE.md (universal entry, including v2 reference vocabulary mechanics at line 48 and direct-API identifier-computation note at line 52); methodology-schema-workstream-plan.md (master); methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide.md (template, section 6 cross-spec consistency requirements and section 7 validation gates); methodology-schema-specs/domain.md v1.0 (first predecessor; source of cross-spec conventions); methodology-schema-specs/entity.md v1.0 (second predecessor; decomposed reference handling, master-pane column deferral); methodology-schema-specs/process.md v1.0 (third predecessor; precedent for justified deviation from cross-spec norms); evolved-methodology-phase-outline.md sections 3 Phase 1 / Phase 3 / Phase 5 (Initial CRM Candidate Set output, persistence across iterations, CRM Selection Decision); db-export/sessions.json snapshots through SES-014 (current state at apply commit 2accd8c); db-export/decisions.json snapshots through DEC-059; crmbuilder-v2/src/crmbuilder_v2/access/vocab.py (current ENTITY_TYPES set, REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS, _kinds_for_pair rules).

Initial pre-flight finding: precondition not met. The schema-design-kickoff-crm_candidate.md predecessor list named domain / entity / process as all required complete before this conversation opens, but the initial repo pull showed only domain.md and entity.md on disk (HEAD at 83c108c). Surfaced this as a flow-stopper with two options: pause and run the process schema-design conversation first, or proceed anyway accepting the cross-spec consistency cost. User responded by running the process conversation in parallel and pushing the results; second pull surfaced process.md v1.0 plus the SES-013 / SES-014 close-out apply commit (HEAD at 2accd8c). Pre-flight resumed and completed with all three predecessors confirmed.

Architectural questions resolved in order:

Q1 — Identifier prefix. Adopted CRM (three letters) under domain.md's soft-3-letter posture; methodology context disambiguates against 'the CRM software' / 'CRM Builder' (the disambiguation supplied by usage context — identifier appearances in UI and methodology documents read as 'CRM-001 (EspoCRM)' or similar). Four-letter CRMC alternative rejected as adding a letter the context already supplies. Decided as routine and announced; no pushback. → DEC-060.

Q2 — Status lifecycle structure. Adopted Model A (four-status enum: active / selected / declined / removed) with three terminal states, an access-layer singleton-selected constraint, default starter 'active'. Deviates from the cross-spec rejection-via-soft-delete principle (DEC-047, DEC-052) by introducing 'removed' as a first-class status value for mid-engagement drops; deviation rationale in spec section 3.4.4. Model B (three-status lifecycle with drop-via-soft-delete) considered and rejected: methodology dynamics of consultant-driven lived-deployment adjustments are meaningfully different from authoring-error rejections, justifying the deviation. Soft-delete preserved for the authoring-error path (record created in error). Three terminal states reflect engagement-event semantics: reversing a Phase 5 outcome or a deliberate mid-engagement removal is a bigger methodology gesture than a status edit should faithfully represent. Singleton-selected constraint enforced at the access layer (HTTP 422 with selected_candidate_already_exists naming the existing record's identifier), matching v2's existing soft-FK pattern. Constraint applies on POST, PATCH/PUT, and restore. UX rough edge acknowledged and logged for CBM-redo-surface: recovering from a fat-fingered transition into 'selected' requires the soft-delete + restore-elsewhere path because terminal states have no transitions out. → DEC-062.

Q3 — Field inventory under minimum-viable v0.4 scope. Adopted Option A (thinnest). Five substantive fields: crm_candidate_identifier (server-assigned, CRM-NNN format), crm_candidate_name (required, engagement-global case-insensitive unique), crm_candidate_fit_reason (required plain text, one-paragraph rationale), crm_candidate_notes (optional consultant scratchpad, plain text), crm_candidate_status (required enum, default 'active' per Q2). No structured classification metadata in v0.4 (vendor_url, hosting_type, license_type, price_tier all deferred to v0.5+ under new PI-012). No storage-level length caps. Inherited timestamps (created_at, updated_at, deleted_at). Option B (Option A + vendor_url) and Option C (full kickoff inventory: vendor_url + three enums) considered and rejected on the minimum-viable principle (Phase 1 produces 2-3 candidates per engagement; structured fields' scannability gain is small relative to schema-UI-migration cost at that count; the Phase 1 selection criteria all live in fit_reason prose naturally; extraction to structured fields later is mechanical, deciding the right enum values is easier with real CBM-redo signal). Parent-prefix field naming inherited from DEC-046 without re-establishment. → DEC-061.

Q4 — Relationship posture and vocabulary additions. No outgoing relationships from crm_candidate in v0.4. No new relationship-kind vocabulary entries registered. Single mechanical addition: crm_candidate added to ENTITY_TYPES in vocab.py (alongside parallel additions of domain, entity, process from this v0.4 release), with Alembic migration extending refs.source_type and refs.target_type CHECK constraints to admit all four new methodology entity types. By virtue of being in ENTITY_TYPES, crm_candidate automatically participates in the universal kinds (is_about, references — valid for any pair), in decided_in (when target is session), and in supersedes (when both source and target are crm_candidate). Existing universal vocabulary covers the kickoff's anticipated 'cited_by_decision' and 'discussed_in_session' semantics without need for new kinds; kickoff's working names were imprecise and the correct names are the existing universal ones. v0.5+ anticipated kinds (deployment_log_targets_candidate, comparison_compares_candidates) deferred along with the entity types that would source them (PI-004 territory). Cross-spec consistency check verified clean: no naming collisions across the four specs' vocab additions. → DEC-063.

Q5 — API surface, UI defaults, singleton-selected enforcement, and acceptance criteria. Standard 8-endpoint set with no deviations from cross-spec defaults. Status-transition validation server-side per section 3.5.3. Singleton-selected enforcement at the access layer on POST / PATCH-PUT / restore per section 3.5.4. Default ListDetailPanel UI under the existing Methodology sidebar group at position #4 (completing the workstream-ordered set: domains, entities, processes, crm_candidates). Master pane columns: identifier / name / status / updated_at. Detail pane: identifier (read-only), name, fit_reason, notes (collapsed under 'Internal notes'), status, ReferencesSection (renders inbound governance-entity citations). Standard create / edit / delete dialogs with delete-dialog clarifying note distinguishing soft-delete (authoring-error path) from status='removed' (legitimate mid-engagement drop) — exact wording deferred to v0.4-build. Master-pane sort behavior for terminal-state records (interleave vs status-first) deferred to v0.4-build. 12 testable acceptance criteria including the singleton-selected enforcement, ENTITY_TYPES registration, and a sample CBM-redo Phase 1 + Phase 5 round-trip. → DEC-064.

Outline of both deliverables proposed and approved; end-to-end drafting executed without per-section confirmation. Spec and v0.4-build-planning kickoff committed to main at 4d5863a (662 insertions across two files). One new planning item authored: PI-012 (crm_candidate structured-metadata enums for v0.5+ pending CBM-redo signal).

Workstream-level closure: this conversation closes the per-entity schema-design portion of the methodology-entity-schema-design workstream. The workstream produced four schema specs (domain.md, entity.md, process.md, crm_candidate.md), nineteen decisions (DEC-044 through DEC-049 from SES-012; DEC-050 through DEC-054 from SES-013; DEC-055 through DEC-059 from SES-014; DEC-060 through DEC-064 from SES-015 pending apply), seven new planning items (PI-006 / PI-007 / PI-008 from SES-012; PI-009 / PI-010 from SES-013; PI-011 from SES-014; PI-012 from SES-015 pending apply), and the companion v0.4-build-planning kickoff. The successor v0.4-build-planning conversation integrates the four specs into the v0.4 release artifacts (PRD, implementation plan, slice build prompts) starting with the cross-spec consistency check per spec guide section 7.2.

The 42-entry base entity catalog (~5,530 rows) now lives in the v2 database as authoritative data; the YAML source files are decommissioned. Commit B: three-pass loader at bootstrap/catalog_loader.py + 13 unit tests against fixture YAMLs. Commit C: Alembic 0004 data migration + git rm of the 42 catalog YAMLs, base-entity-catalog-research.md, and entity-system-map.yaml + integration test (auto-skips once YAMLs are gone). Commit D: catalog Pydantic request schemas. Commit E: access/repositories/catalog/read.py (list/get/get_attribute/search/cross_system_map/gap_check) + 6 REST read endpoints + 27 tests. Commit F: write.py (8 entity+attribute write methods, soft-delete, nested-payload replacement) + 8 REST write endpoints + 29 tests. Commit G: 4 read-only MCP tools + 12 tests. Commit H: per-entity JSON exports under db-export/catalog/entities/ (42 files), export hook fired from all write methods with a suppression() context manager, bulk regenerate; universal references integration - catalog_entity/catalog_attribute added to ENTITY_TYPES + Alembic 0005 extends the refs and change_log CHECK constraints + inbound_references surfaced on entity/attribute detail responses + 17 tests. summary: The conversation opened against a kickoff prompt that imagined methodology entity schema as unplanned greenfield needing a full PRD-implementation-plan-Claude-Code-prompt deliverable set. Inspection of the repo surfaced the actual state: the methodology-entity-schema-design workstream had already produced four schema specs, a workstream plan, a spec guide v1.1 amendment, a draft v0.4 PRD, a draft implementation plan, six build prompts, and four close-out payloads. The kickoff was stale by ~5 days. A reconciliation brief was produced laying out three options: ignore the kickoff and pick up the in-flight workstream (Option A), treat the kickoff as authoritative (Option B), or reconcile by mapping the kickoff's scope against the workstream's then deciding (Option C). Option C was selected. A second pass through ui-PRD-v0.4, the four schema specs, the implementation plan, six build prompts, decisions.json (DEC-038 through DEC-067), and sessions.json (SES-001 through SES-016) confirmed v0.4 was approval-ready with renumbering deltas. PRD §11 and the implementation plan §8 were updated to reflect the renumbering. Three previously-untracked planning items were added to PRD §2 Out of Scope: PI-013 Cross-Domain Service representation, PI-014 Catalog FK integration for methodology entities, PI-015 Methodology entity renderers. Nine files were committed as the approval pass. After slice A completed, the `run-v0.4-slice.sh` launcher was authored to streamline slice B–F invocation. After slice C completed, PI-016 was identified as a recommended candidate (router-level per-pair vocab enforcement on `/references`). After slice E completed, the gap that per-slice session record authoring had been buried in F-closeout step 2 rather than inlined in each slice prompt was surfaced and this drafts file produced as the recovery path.

One finding worth carrying forward: criterion 14's "POST /references with (entity, domain) and an unsupported kind returns 422" check is exercised in this slice via strict-schema rejection of a body keyed `relationship_kind` (rather than the wire key `kind`), matching the prompt's literal example. This gap is the basis for PI-016 (recommended new planning item — see Section 3).
